extraction.one_line_summary: QUINTANA Nairo attacked one-third up the final climb (roughly 14km from finish at 7000+ feet altitude), dropped everyone, won his first Tour stage since 2013.
tactical_patterns: QUINTANA attacked from bottom of final climb after mechanical/wheel change, using altitude advantage to leapfrog multiple groups—gutsy early commitment paid off.
VALVERDE Alejandro in early break to serve QUINTANA when he bridged—Movistar planted domestique preemptively for long-range mountain attack.
THOMAS Geraint took third for 4-second time bonus on mountaintop finish, prioritizing gap management over chasing stage win—risk-averse yellow jersey defense.
MARTIN Dan chased QUINTANA solo after bridging through groups, finishing second—no team support creates near-miss pattern on mountain stages for him.

rider_signals.QUINTANA Nairo: positive: First Tour stage win since 2013 shows high-altitude steep pitches (14-15% gradient, 7000+ feet) remain his singular advantage when fresh.
rider_signals.THOMAS Geraint: positive: Controlled climb for third place with time bonus, nearly 2-minute lead now—looks psychologically confident after yesterday's visible tension.
rider_signals.DUMOULIN Tom: positive: Finished fifth, moved to second overall—quietly consistent performance on steep altitude climb where he normally suffers.
rider_signals.FROOME Chris: negative: Lost 1:35 on final climb, dropped to third at 2:31 back—struggling on steep gradients suggests fading podium threat rather than threat to THOMAS.
rider_signals.MARTIN Dan: positive: Bridged solo through multiple groups to finish second after early deficit—exceptional individual pursuit ability without team support on mountains.
rider_signals.VALVERDE Alejandro: positive: Planted in breakaway specifically to assist QUINTANA's long-range attack—tactical MVP shows veteran positioning intelligence at 38 years old.
rider_signals.LANDA Mikel: neutral: Only rider who briefly held QUINTANA's wheel before being dropped on upper slopes—pace reference point for elite climbers.
rider_signals.BERNAL Egan: positive: Strong performance at 21 years old mentioned as future Sky leader—already riding at GC contender level in third week mountains.
rider_signals.ROGLIČ Primož: neutral: Finished close to THOMAS/DUMOULIN group, maintaining podium contention—consistent presence without breakthrough or collapse.
rider_signals.ALAPHILIPPE Julian: positive: Extended polka dot lead to 67 points with eighth consecutive day in jersey—lock on classification before final mountain stages.
